items.prompt: computes and interprets center and spread measures and compares bounded data distributions from plots and tables
items.expected_evidence: compare distributions using both numerical summaries and distribution features
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner selects and interprets appropriate summaries while preserving the structure of the full distribution.

items.prompt: explains how center, spread, shape, and outliers together describe a distribution and when different summaries are more useful
items.expected_evidence: justify why a chosen summary is informative for the distribution at hand
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains distributions as structured data sets rather than lists reduced to one number.

items.prompt: Diagnose a peer error aligned to outlier_summary_blindness.
items.expected_evidence: compute and interpret mean, median, range, interquartile range, and mean absolute deviation in bounded cases
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies how a chosen summary masked key distribution features or was distorted by outliers.

items.prompt: interprets and compares distributions in a less-familiar data context and checks whether the statistical claim is actually supported
items.expected_evidence: describe the shape and variability of bounded data distributions from plots or tables
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves distribution meaning across representation and claim context.
